text: The good:- The new controller is awesome! I recommend playing the free Astro's Playroom tech demo game that shows off all the features. That game is way better than it has any right to be and is a great introduction to the controller's capabilities. I hope lots of future games utilize the DualSense controller to the same extent.- Backwards compatible with PS4 games. I have a large library of physical PS4 games, so it's great that I get to still enjoy them on the PS5 with improved performance. Some PS4 games like FF7 Remake even include a free PS5 upgrade. It was easy to migrate my games and save files from my PS4 with an external drive and PS Plus cloud saves.- Fantastic performance. The load times are incredibly fast on most modern games.The bad:- WiFi connection is noticeably weaker than on the Xbox One X. I don't recommend using the online features unless you're using a wired connection. Even stuff like buying games in the PS Store is needlessly slow and unstable.- No themes or wallpapers. I liked to customize my PS4 with themes and custom wallpapers. Unfortunately the PS5 does not support that as of the writing of this review, so you're stuck with the boring default user interface.- Lack of exclusive games. Currently the PS5 does not have enough console-specific exclusives to make it stand out in today's gaming scene. The Demon's Souls remake and Ratchet and Clank Rift Apart are excellent, but we need more. I know we're also going to get FFXVI soon, but I doubt it will stay as a console exclusive for long.Overall:This is a great console, but I only recommend buying it for the backwards compatibility for gamers who own a lot of PS4 games. It's also worth getting if you don't already own a good gaming PC or an Xbox One X and want to play some next-gen games.
location_and_date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2023

text: When I first unboxed the PS5, I was honestly wowed by how big the system was... And since I was aware that the PS5 is an absolute UNIT of a system when I bought it, I pre-emptively made room for it in my setup before it arrived.The PS5 system DWARFS my Wii U in sheer size, and it has quite the commanding presence in my gaming setup, as seen in the picture I've provided.Regardless... Given that the PS5 seamlessly plays PS4 games, I can now dive into a bunch of PS4-era games I missed out on, like Dreams, LittleBigPlanet 3, and WipEout Omega Collection.In regards to the PS5's own games, the only game I've had the chance to truly dive into was Armored Core 6: Fires of Rubicon, a multi-platform game that I wound up starting to hyper-fixate on... But that's for another review.I've briefly played a few other PS5 games I bought alongside my system, and they look and play beautifully on my 4K 120hz Sony TV after configuring the PS5 to use 120hz and VRR.The DualSense controller the system comes with is highly responsive, and I find that the adaptive triggers feel trippy in a good way when they kick in, as games can change how the triggers feel depending on the context and what you're doing.The PS5 comes with Astro's Playroom installed, which is a short and sweet collect-a-thon platformer that lovingly references the history of the PlayStation brand all throughout, including little robots cosplaying as characters that made their mark on PlayStation history. I got a kick out of feeling the triggers resisting against my pushing in the controller demo that the game includes, to be honest.The home menu UI took me a bit to figure out at first, but it's easy to navigate and looks fairly slick. However, one disappointment I'd like to air out is that the PS5 doesn't have any themes, which the PS4 apparently had.In conclusion, the PS5's an overall great system, and I can't wait to dive into the games I got for it!
location_and_date: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2023

text: The PlayStation 5 is an absolute powerhouse! From the moment I unboxed it, I was blown away by its sleek design and futuristic look. The performance is top-tier, with lightning-fast load times thanks to the SSD, and the graphics are stunning—games run smoothly in 4K with beautiful detail and fluid frame rates.What really sets the PS5 apart is the DualSense controller. The adaptive triggers and haptic feedback add an immersive touch that makes every game feel more engaging, whether you're drawing a bow or feeling terrain shifts under your virtual car's tires. Backward compatibility is another plus, letting me enjoy my PS4 library with better performance.The interface is clean and easy to navigate, and features like game hints and the ability to jump between activities make gaming more seamless than ever. Whether you're into single-player adventures or online multiplayer, the PS5 is a game-changer. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for the ultimate gaming experience!
location_and_date: Reviewed in the United States on October 18, 2024
